chunk_summary: This section discusses immunotherapy and biologic counseling boundaries in food allergy management, emphasizing the role of therapies like Xolair (omalizumab) alongside avoidance strategies, nutrition planning, and action plans for children.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of biologic therapies, such as Xolair, in food allergy management.
Learn counseling strategies for integrating immunotherapy with avoidance and nutrition planning.
Explore emerging guidelines for eosinophilic esophagitis management in children.
Evaluate FDA-approved therapies and their limitations in food allergy treatment.
key_facts: Xolair (omalizumab) is FDA-approved for food allergy management as of 2025.
Biologic therapies reduce risk but do not replace avoidance strategies or action plans.
Eosinophilic esophagitis management guidelines are detailed in ESPGHAN's 2024 publication.
Counseling scripts should emphasize the importance of nutrition planning alongside therapy.
FDA highlights Xolair's role in selected pediatric cases, requiring specialist oversight.
reference_quote: New therapies can reduce risk for selected children, but they do not replace avoidance decisions, nutrition planning, and action plans. (FDA, 2025)
document_key_topics: FDA-approved therapies for pediatric food allergies
chunk_heading: FOOD ALLERGY & THERAPEUTIC ELIMINATION DIETS;EMERGING THERAPIES AND SPECIALIST OPTIONS IN FOOD ALLERGY;IMMUNOTHERAPY AND BIOLOGIC COUNSELING BOUNDARIES;
subtopic: EMERGING THERAPIES AND SPECIALIST OPTIONS IN FOOD ALLERGY
